title: Used BMW 3 Series (F30) Buying Guide: Risks & What to Check | AutoVerdict
description: What to check before buying a used BMW 3 Series F30 — timing chain on diesels, cooling system, oil leaks, run-flat tyres, and seller questions.
intro: The F30 3 Series is a rewarding used buy, but rear-wheel-drive premium engineering brings its own checklist. Here is what to verify before you arrange a viewing.

faq.q: Is the BMW 3 Series F30 reliable?
faq.a: The F30 (2012–2019) is generally a strong used car, especially when maintained by someone who understands BMWs. The areas most worth checking are the diesel timing chain on earlier engines, the cooling system, and minor oil leaks that are common with age.

faq.q: Which F30 engine has timing chain issues?
faq.a: Earlier N47 diesel engines gained a reputation for timing chain wear, with the chain at the back of the engine making repairs costly. Later B47 diesels addressed this. On any diesel F30, listen for chain rattle on cold start and ask about any chain work.

faq.q: Do I need to worry about run-flat tyres?
faq.a: Many F30s came on run-flat tyres and without a spare wheel. Run-flats are more expensive and give a firmer ride. Check tyre condition and brand match, and factor replacement cost into your budget.
